mnemonics: Caesar's Substitution Cipher: Think 'Shift 3' for Caesar's name (C=3rd letter) and his method of shifting letters
keyPoints: Substitution Ciphers replace plaintext with ciphertext
Transposition Ciphers rearrange the order of plaintext
Modern encryption relies on complex mathematical algorithms and keys
commonMistakes: Assuming simple substitution ciphers are secure for modern applications
Using easily guessable keys (like simple words or dates)
